command: cargo run -q -p nose-cli -- verify crates --max-violations 0 --recall-loss-report target/recall-loss.issue-587-after.json
goal: Separate unsupported/non-value import snapshot boundaries from same-repo Rust module/export lookup, while opening only dependency-backed immutable literal exports.
implemented_capabilities: Rust crate-root module identity now recognizes src/lib.rs, src/main.rs, and mod.rs ownership for imported immutable literal provider lookup.
Rust importer-relative module lookup now derives self:: and super:: aliases from provider/importer file identities before matching an immutable literal export.
The import snapshot census now reports callable, type, module namespace, Rust stdlib, and workspace-crate boundaries separately from generic provider-module-missing/provider-export-missing.
Non-value Rust exports remain closed; only provider-owned literal-safe assignments can create ImportedLiteralSnapshot evidence.
notes: The after unresolved_binding_imports count includes new focused Rust test fixtures and copied import-dependency surfaces; the durable #587 progress metric for this slice is the generic module/export target rows, which moved 378 -> 139.
The remaining generic provider-export-missing rows are mostly re-export or unsupported export-shape cases and should be priced separately before admitting new snapshot forms.
validation: cargo run -q -p nose-cli -- verify crates --max-violations 0 --recall-loss-report target/recall-loss.issue-587-after.json
